text.title: BA Methodologies Detail — 12 Enterprise Frameworks
text.summary: Purpose and four-step process for each of the 12 BA frameworks: governance through solution limitation assessment.

section.title: 1. Governance Framework
section.p: Purpose: establish decision-making processes for requirements. Defines who can approve, who can escalate, and how changes are controlled.

rule.statement: Define decision rights, change control process, prioritization rules, and approval documentation before any requirements are baselined.
rule.rationale: Without governance, scope changes are approved ad hoc and no one can explain why a requirement was added or removed; audit trails collapse.

section.reference: Decision authority matrix and change control process template

section.title: 2. Communication Planning
section.p: Purpose: ensure effective communication of BA information to the right audiences in the right format and frequency.

rule.statement: Identify audience, information need, format, frequency, and channel for each stakeholder group before the project begins. Validate the plan at each phase.
rule.rationale: Without a communication plan, critical information reaches the wrong people at the wrong time; sponsors get developer-level detail and developers get executive summaries.

section.reference: Audience matrix and key messages template

section.title: 3. Elicitation Preparation
section.p: Purpose: prepare effectively for elicitation activities by defining scope, selecting techniques, and preparing materials before sessions begin.

list.item: Define scope — what information is needed, what decisions will be made, what is out of scope.
Select techniques — match technique to information type and stakeholder preferences.
Prepare logistics — schedule sessions, book resources, prepare environment.
Create materials — question guides, visual aids, prototypes or mockups.

section.title: 4. Requirements Maintenance
section.p: Purpose: keep requirements accurate and useful over the full project lifecycle through version control, attribute management, and periodic quality monitoring.

list.item: Version control — baseline management, change history tracking.
Attribute management — status (draft/approved/implemented), priority, owner, source.
Quality monitoring — periodic reviews, consistency checks, completeness validation.
Archival strategy — when to archive, what to retain, access policies.

section.title: 5. Change Impact Analysis
section.p: Purpose: assess the scope, effort, risk, and stakeholder impact of a proposed requirement change before it is approved or rejected.

rule.statement: Complete all five impact dimensions — scope, effort, risk, stakeholder impact, and decision support — before recommending accept, reject, or defer.
rule.rationale: Partial impact analysis leads to under-scoped change requests; effort or risk assessments without the other dimensions produce recommendations that surprise the team at implementation time.

section.reference: Change impact analysis template with scope, effort, risks, recommendation

section.title: 6. Current State Analysis
section.p: Purpose: understand the existing environment — business context, capabilities, technology landscape, stakeholders, and pain points — before defining the future state.

rule.statement: Require a context-loading step (document analysis, transcript ingest) before running any current-state framework. An agent cannot produce a valid SWOT for a company it knows nothing about.
rule.rationale: LLMs generate plausible-but-wrong current-state descriptions when no ground truth is provided; hallucinated baselines produce strategies that address problems the organization does not have.

section.reference: Current state assessment: business context, capability assessment, SWOT

section.title: 7. Future State Definition
section.p: Purpose: define the desired future state through vision statement, SMART goals, required capabilities, success metrics, and constraints.

rule.statement: Every success metric must have a baseline value and a target value. Metrics without baselines cannot prove improvement.
rule.rationale: Agents fabricate plausible industry-standard KPIs when not required to cite sources; require a datasource path for every metric and reject metrics without baselines.

section.reference: Future state vision: goals, capability roadmap, constraints, assumptions

section.title: 8. Risk Analysis
section.p: Purpose: identify and assess risks to the change initiative using probability-impact scoring and define response strategies.

rule.statement: Attach an evidence column to every risk (incident history, expert judgment, similar project, none). Reject high-impact risks with evidence: none in reviews.
rule.rationale: Probability and impact scores without evidence are guesses; agents consistently assign P/I scores based on plausibility rather than evidence, producing a risk register that looks complete but contains no actionable signal.

section.reference: Risk register with probability, impact, score, response, owner

section.title: 9. Change Strategy Planning
section.p: Purpose: develop the optimal approach for achieving the future state through gap analysis, solution options, transition planning, readiness assessment, and business case.

rule.statement: Map every identified gap to a named solution option before scoring options. Options not mapped to gaps are feature proposals, not strategy.
rule.rationale: Solution options analysis without explicit gap linkage produces a preference list rather than a strategy; the recommendation becomes subjective rather than traceable to the change rationale.

section.reference: Gap analysis, solution options, and transition roadmap template

section.title: 10. Requirements Architecture
section.p: Purpose: organize requirements into a coherent structure through viewpoints (business/user/technical/operational), decomposition hierarchy, dependency mapping, and completeness check.
section.reference: Viewpoints, requirement hierarchy, and dependency table template

section.title: 11. Solution Options Analysis
section.p: Purpose: evaluate and recommend the best solution option using weighted criteria scoring across strategic fit, technical feasibility, cost, time to value, and risk.

rule.statement: Calibrate scoring weights with human stakeholders before the agent applies them. Return recommendation as PENDING_HUMAN_REVIEW until explicit go-ahead is given.
rule.rationale: Agents score every option 3-4 to appear neutral, destroying discriminating power. The 25/20/20/20/15 default weights are illustrative; sponsors must declare their actual weights before the scores are meaningful.

section.reference: Weighted scoring matrix and recommendation template

section.title: 12. Solution Limitation Assessment
section.p: Purpose: identify and address current solution limitations through defect identification, root cause analysis, impact assessment, and remediation options evaluation.
section.reference: Limitation identification, root cause, impact, and remediation template
